summary:
The painful socket likely indicates an episode of alveolar osteitis ("dry socket"). Treatment involves irrigating the area with sterile water or antiseptic solution followed by placement of a medicated gauze packed locally for analgesia and healing promotion as per standard protocol options available including "Option-A".
The recommended course includes cleaning out debris from the empty tooth socket using gentle rinsing techniques then applying soothing dressings designed specifically address such conditions effectively reducing discomfort while promoting recovery process naturally over time. To ensure proper care follow up closely monitoring progress ensuring adequate relief achieved before discharge home instructions given accordingly based upon individual needs assessed clinically during visit today. ```plaintext
The painful socket suggests possible alveolar osteitis ('dry socket'). Treat it by gently irrigating the socket with saline or chlorhexidine, then placing a medicated dressing that provides both cleansing and numbing effects.
```
